originalTitle: OpenAI files for IPO, following Anthropic
summary: OpenAI on Monday checked off a preliminary step in the IPO race that it and rival Anthropic have been competing in for the better part of a year: The company announced it has confidentially submitted a Form S-1 with the US Securities and Exchange Commission, following Anthropic's decision to do the same on June 1st. […]
content: OpenAI on Monday checked off a preliminary step in the IPO race that it and rival Anthropic have been competing in for the better part of a year: The company announced it has confidentially submitted a Form S-1 with the US Securities and Exchange Commission, following Anthropic’s decision to do the same on June 1st.  The confidential filing means that certain details normally available through the form — such as executive compensation figures, potential risks to a company’s business, and more financials — aren’t yet public.  As of Anthropic’s most recent fundraise, it’s being called the world’s most valuable startup, with a post-money valuation of $965 billion that surpassed OpenAI’s latest $852 billion post-money valuation.  OpenAI has been preparing to go public for months, but reports have surfaced that certain executives, namely CFO Sarah Friar, haven’t been as gung-ho about the fast-tracked IPO as CEO Sam Altman, due to missed revenue targets and user growth numbers, and concerns that OpenAI won’t be able to pay for all its compute spending commitments. The company had initially said it was planning to spend $1.4 trillion on compute infrastructure, which Altman seemed to become defensive about when publicly questioned on it. In February, though, OpenAI adjusted that figure, telling investors it plans to spend $600 billion on compute by 2030. The news also comes weeks after the jury reached a verdict in the high-profile Musk v. Altman trial, and ahead of Musk-owned SpaceX’s planned June 12 IPO. SpaceX’s public debut is currently set to raise $80 billion and become the biggest IPO of all time. OpenAI’s own debut will be very publicly compared to that of SpaceX, especially since SpaceX acquired OpenAI competitor xAI and signed a deal with Anthropic, with Anthropic paying $15 billion a year to use SpaceX data centers.
chineseTitle: 继 Anthropic 之后，OpenAI 申请 IPO
chineseSummary: OpenAI 周一检查了其与竞争对手 Anthropic 一年来的 IPO 竞赛中的初步步骤：该公司宣布已秘密向美国证券交易委员会提交 S-1 表格，此前 Anthropic 于 6 月 1 日也决定这样做。 […]
chineseContent: OpenAI 周一检查了其与竞争对手 Anthropic 一年来的 IPO 竞赛中的初步步骤：该公司宣布已向美国证券交易委员会秘密提交 S-1 表格，此前 Anthropic 于 6 月 1 日也决定这样做。  机密文件意味着通常通过表格提供的某些详细信息（例如高管薪酬数据、公司业务的潜在风险以及更多财务信息）尚未公开。  截至 Anthropic 最近一次融资，它被称为世界上最有价值的初创公司，投后估值达到 9650 亿美元，超过了 OpenAI 最新的 8520 亿美元投后估值。  OpenAI 几个月来一直在准备上市，但有报道称，某些高管，即首席财务官 Sarah Friar，并没有像首席执行官 Sam Altman 那样热衷于快速 IPO，因为未能实现收入目标和用户增长数字，并且担心 OpenAI 将无法支付其所有计算支出承诺。该公司最初表示计划斥资 1.4 万亿美元用于计算基础设施，但当公开质疑时，奥特曼似乎对此采取了防御态度。不过，2 月份，OpenAI 调整了这一数字，告诉投资者计划到 2030 年在计算领域投入 6000 亿美元。这一消息发布的几周前，陪审团在备受瞩目的马斯克诉奥特曼案审判中做出了裁决，而马斯克旗下的 SpaceX 计划于 6 月 12 日进行 IPO。 SpaceX 的首次公开募股目前预计筹集 800 亿美元，成为有史以来规模最大的 IPO。与 SpaceX 相比，OpenAI 自己的首次亮相将会非常公开，特别是自从 SpaceX 收购了 OpenAI 竞争对手 xAI 并与 Anth 签署了协议之后
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
